content:
* We acknowledge that we are gathered on the traditional and unceded territory of the Algonquin Anishinaabe Nation. For generations, they have been the stewards of this land, and their deep connection to it continues to this day. We recognize the enduring presence and contributions of the Algonquin people to this region. We also acknowledge the painful history of colonization and the displacement of Indigenous communities from their ancestral lands. We commit ourselves to learning, understanding, and honoring the Algonquin Nation's history and culture. We recognize the importance of working collaboratively to create a more just and equitable future for all who call this land home.
________________
sentences: * We acknowledge that we are gathered on the traditional and unceded territory of the Algonquin Anishinaabe Nation.
For generations, they have been the stewards of this land, and their deep connection to it continues to this day.
We recognize the enduring presence and contributions of the Algonquin people to this region.
We also acknowledge the painful history of colonization and the displacement of Indigenous communities from their ancestral lands.
We commit ourselves to learning, understanding, and honoring the Algonquin Nation's history and culture.

We recognize the importance of working collaboratively to create a more just and equitable future for all who call this land home.
________________

content:
* Attended Canadian Engineering Leadership Conference (CELC) 2026 at Western University with four delegates, reimbursements being processed, invoice paid and delegates will provide their take backs for January BOD
* Preparations in place to attend the Conference on Advocacy and Leadership in Engineering (CALE) 2026 at McMaster University, delegates selected and registered, transportation booked, delegate meeting planned
* Applications for Conference on Sustainability in Engineering (CSE) 2026 at ETS closed, delegate selection in progress, need to register before January 22nd
* Planning the Carleton x uOttawa event - dates finalized, planning in progress
* Preparing PM preliminary-bid for January BOD, to hopefully be presented at CALE 2026

content:
* Trying to push promo for eng ball - Early bird tix sold out but regular ticket sales are very slow
* Please make sure to promo it during office hrs!!! If you see people buy, remind them to fill out the google form in their emails
* Some auction items in, extra budget from cssa will go to more items if sponsors don’t accept :)
* Carty House representative will be attending eng ball
* Invited to bug push but forgot to budget for that in sept - work in progress
* Formal clothing drive didn’t end up going through! Not enough interest from other rsgs or donations from thrift stores -> still enough potential to be an event next year though :)

content:
* Month of Francophony event: Plan to organize a Champions League watchparty taking inspiration from AFCON and World Cup watchparties. Focus would be on Francophone teams with French commentary. The date would be between the 23-25th awaiting the matchups to be revealed Jan 30th.
* Will help Luke with Franco merch for this term, I believe the deadline we wanna hit is reading week.
* Franco Round Table is organized every Monday at 7pm too so I will send Hayden to substitute for me when it happens.
* Plans to motion to remove the position of Francophone Outreach.

* Office Hours Guests - President (Reminder)
* Eric: Starting next semester we will limit to only 1 friend / non-officer while in the office  (esp if it’s not your OH). As we become a larger and more active team, it is difficult to work in a busy environment that has become almost too welcoming to outsiders.
* VPFA: When can we charge winter intramural fees?

content:
* WHEREAS Spirit Commissioner was mentioned at some point I don’t remember to be added as a trial basis during the Winter Term
WHEREAS the Spirit Commissioner’s goal is create monthly challenges for 101 week teams to carry the energy from the week on to the rest of the year.
BE IT RESOLVED THAT Guillaume Mizon is brought in as Spirit Commissioner for 2026 Winter Term
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED THAT if there is general content with the position, it would possibly carry on and will take on the roles of 101 Week Coordinator
* Essentially, (On the BIFR) the 101 week coordinator will continue throughout the year.
* For: 14; Against: 0; Abstain: 2
* WHEREAS VP Communications has requested for 1.5 office hours.
BE IT RESOLVED THAT Hayley Jubinville has 1.5 office hours for the 2026 Winter Term.
* Unanimous consent
sentences:
WHEREAS Spirit Commissioner was mentioned at some point I don’t remember to be added as a trial basis during the Winter Term
WHEREAS the Spirit Commissioner’s goal is create monthly challenges for 101 week teams to carry the energy from the week on to the rest of the year.

BE IT RESOLVED THAT Guillaume Mizon is brought in as Spirit Commissioner for 2026 Winter Term
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED THAT if there is general content with the position, it would possibly carry on and will take on the roles of 101 Week Coordinator
*
Essentially, (On the BIFR) the 101 week coordinator will continue throughout the year.

* For: 14; Against: 0; Abstain: 2
* WHEREAS VP Communications has requested for 1.5 office hours.
BE IT RESOLVED THAT Hayley Jubinville has 1.5 office hours for the 2026 Winter Term.
* Unanimous consent
